model_narrative: We are mapping the shortest sea routes for a world phosphate network. From each source port we send flow out across a network of directed sea links, where every link carries a known distance in nautical miles. For each source we choose how much flow to push along each directed link. The goal is to make the total routed length, summed over every source and every link, as small as possible.

sets.name: nodes
sets.doc: the full set of ports / nodes in the sea-route network

sets.name: src
sets.doc: the source nodes; the origins that flow is routed out from

sets.name: dest
sets.doc: the destination nodes that must be reached from the sources

sets.name: arcs
sets.doc: the directed arcs that carry a distance; each physical link is stored both ways, so (n, np) and (np, n) are separate members

params.name: darc
params.doc: the distance of each directed arc, in nautical miles

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the amount of flow sent from a given source along a given directed arc

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: the total routed length over all sources and arcs, in nautical miles

constraints.name: x[s,
constraints.intent: For each source and for every node that is not that source itself, the total flow from that source arriving into the node must be at least one unit greater than the total flow from that source leaving the node. A source node imposes no such requirement on itself.

constraints.name: cost
constraints.intent: The total routed length accounts for every bit of flow on every link. Across all sources and all directed links, take the distance of each link times the flow that source sends along it, and set the total length equal to the sum of all those amounts.

query: There's a proposal on the table to ease congestion at the two Gulf gateways veracruz leans on first: keep the combined first-leg flow it pushes straight onto the mobile arc and the yucatan-ch arc to no more than 40 units between them. With that ceiling on those two near-shore legs taken together, what does the optimizer return for the total route length?

query: We're looking at a balancing rule for the two western lanes leaving yucatan-ch. The board wants the s-gibraltr leg held to no more than 30 percent of the flow on those two western legs combined — that is, of the s-gibraltr first-hop plus the panama first-hop added together. Plug that share limit in and walk me through the new total routing distance.

query: Engineering is pitching a two-part rerouting package out of veracruz, and we want to see both pieces applied together. First, hold the hand-off it sends straight to yucatan-ch down to at most 40 units; and at the same time keep the flow it puts directly onto the panama arc at no more than 21 units. With both of those legs reined in at once, what total route length does the model come back with?

query: Both source trees pour a fat 19-unit stream off s-gibraltr onward into alexandria — every origin leans on that exact same Mediterranean leg just as hard. Shouldn't each source be made to thin that s-gibraltr-to-alexandria leg down to 10 at most instead of all funneling through it identically? What's the model seeing that keeps it loading that one onward leg the same way for every origin?

query: Out of veracruz the plan shoves 54 units down the short yucatan-ch hand-off but only 21 onto the panama lane — it looks lopsided toward that one near-shore hop. Couldn't we insist the direct panama leg carry at least as much as the yucatan-ch hand-off does, so the two aren't so skewed? What's the tradeoff that's pulling it so hard toward the yucatan-ch side?

query: Every source dumps a big slug of first-leg flow straight onto its own panama arc — it's the same heavy reliance on the Panama gateway from each origin. Wouldn't it be obvious to hold each source's direct panama first-hop down to 12 across the board? What's keeping the optimizer from spreading that initial load off panama for every origin?

query: Look at how much routing yucatan-ch crams onto its two western lanes at once — 21 units straight to panama and another 27 onto s-gibraltr. That's nearly fifty units leaving on just those two first-hops. Couldn't we just hold the panama and s-gibraltr legs to 35 between the two of them and let the rest of the tree carry more? What's the model seeing that has it leaning on those two western lanes so heavily together?
